//notes: while it does have a massive Velcro area at the bottom, it's likely going to be used by a QASM placard

since there's no multimodality anywhere in the game, we can't use the bottom Velcro panel as EITHER a QASM placard (which would stick its back on the Velcro after connecting with the buckles) OR a Velcro panel (for patches or other Velcro attachments, perhaps player-made)
earlier reviews/advertisements of the LPSPC also show the top Velcro panel hiding an admin pouch underneath
this no longer appears to be the case in the model available at the time of release of this mod
description: A minimal and lightweight plate carrier.  Barebones on its own, it can be extended by a variety of pouches and other parts, or used in conjunction with a chest rig.  This plate carrier doesn't offer protection of its own, as it doesn't feature any stab- or bullet-resistant inserts; you can insert ballistic plates to make it effective against gunfire.

variants.description: The Low Profile Slick Plate Carrier, minimal and lightweight on its own.  Integrated QASM interface and a large hook-and-loop surface allow for attaching placards and chassis, as well as extending the functionality of the carrier with a variety of aftermarket hook-and-loop pouch options.  The LPSPC doesn't feature or allow for soft ballistic inserts due to its low-profile design, but could be enhanced with hard plates.  <variant:multicam>

//weight: there appear to be no sources available on the weight of this particular model
however, given that this model is designed to be slick, we can extrapolate its weight from similar models on the market
for example, LBT Inc. low-profile plate carriers designed for medium SAPI plates start at 1.54 lbs ≈ 699 g
besides, reviews describe it as lightweight
we're going to assume this as the weight of LPSPC until a better source becomes available

notes: width is supposed to be equal to the width of the lower Velcro surface (10.25 in.), which stretches from edge to edge
other dimensions acquired from visual reference
length (thickness) is double the single-panel measurement (3 mm × 2 + 0.1 mm × 2 × 2)
see `armor` for further explanation of thickness

//longest_side: review says this can fold very compactly
for now, we leave it as unfolded height, and adjust from testing if necessary
//armor: it does not appear as though this plate carrier provides any sort of ballistic protection on its own
one of the reviews/advertisements presents it as
which refers to a Class 3 inside the spec, not NIJ III-level ballistic protection
(even then, the description is wrong: class 3 in that spec means 'dyed olive', while the carrier is clearly coyote/tan)
the rest of Class 3 in that standard defines material density and fire-/waterproofness qualities of the material, which may have been what this references

//notes: this is supposed to be spacer mesh made of polyester

//coverage: medium SAPI plate upper torso coverage: 34%

//notes: this is supposed to be spacer mesh made of polyester

//coverage: medium SAPI plate lower torso coverage: 10%

pocket_data.//max_contains_weight: while there's no clear indication as to any maximum weight for the potential interfaces of this sort, we err on the side of caution and use a low weight limit

//flags: NORMAL layer since this carrier meant to be worn under outerwear

//notes: this item was supposed to be modelled after MTV in the base game
the MTV does not have deltoid (shoulder) protectors, while IOTV (still in service) does
for consistency's sake, and because reality still affords us the opportunity, we're modelling this item after the IOTV Gen III vest
additionally, this item is modelled after size M, mostly for consistency
UCP use was discontinued in 2019
description: A long-standing and field-tested, though not particularly beloved, design of a standard-issue plate carrier for the U.S. Army.  It can withstand small-caliber rounds and shrapnel due to embedded Kevlar inserts, and its protection could be further improved by adding ballistic plates.  Additionally, it can be augmented with shoulder, groin, lower-back, and neck protectors.

variants.description: A third-generation design of an Improved Outer Tactical Vest, itself an improvement over the older OTV plate carrier.  Uses soft-armor inserts for protection against pistol-caliber rounds and shrapnel, with hard-armor inserts enabling further protection against rifle-caliber fire.  In addition to that, it can be augmented with a set of soft-armor outserts for the groin, shoulder, lower back, and neck/nape areas.  <variant:ocp>

A third-generation design of an Improved Outer Tactical Vest, itself an improvement over the older OTV plate carrier.  Uses soft-armor inserts for protection against pistol-caliber rounds and shrapnel, with hard-armor inserts enabling further protection against rifle-caliber fire.  In addition to that, it can be augmented with a set of soft-armor outserts for the groin, shoulder, lower back, and neck/nape areas.  <variant:ucp>

A third-generation design of an Improved Outer Tactical Vest, itself an improvement over the older OTV plate carrier.  Uses soft-armor inserts for protection against pistol-caliber rounds and shrapnel, with hard-armor inserts enabling further protection against rifle-caliber fire.  In addition to that, it can be augmented with a set of soft-armor outserts for the groin, shoulder, lower back, and neck/nape areas.  <variant:coyote>

//: should not spawn in the wild
variants.description: A third-generation design of an Improved Outer Tactical Vest, itself an improvement over the older OTV plate carrier.  Uses soft-armor inserts for protection against pistol-caliber rounds and shrapnel, with hard-armor inserts enabling further protection against rifle-caliber fire.  In addition to that, it can be augmented with a set of soft-armor outserts for the groin, shoulder, lower back, and neck/nape areas.  <variant:black>

//weight: reference material provides IOTV weight as base vest (this item) + 2 ballistic panels
we presume these panels to be soft, Kevlar panels
we also presume (at least at this stage) that these panels are embedded into the vest and are not replaceable

notes: length (thickness) is taken from Perroz LPSPC
other dimensions acquired from visual reference
in IOTV reference, front width likely refers to width of IOTV with cummerbunds laid out ouside
we assume, for ease of calculations, that cummerbunds are tucked in, without making too much of a difference for other dimensions

//thickness: conforms to IOTV spec (max. thickness of ballistic protection system 0.3 in.)
this is likely incorrect, but no clearer data seem to be available

//coverage: approximated from large ballistic plate coverage (38% per front/back)

material.//thickness: 0.2 mm × 2 sides per end of the carrier

//thickness: conforms to IOTV spec (max. thickness of ballistic protection system 0.3 in.)
this is likely incorrect, but no clearer data seem to be available

pocket_data.description: Attachment points for a shoulder protector
Attachment points for a shoulder protector
Attachment points for a groin protector
Attachment points for a neck protector
Attachment points for a lower back protector
Cummerbund Quad Release system attachment point

//notes: modelled after size M
there appears to be two parts of the MSV: a Tier I vest (to which cummerbunds strap?) and a Tier III vest (which has the webbing and the plate pouches)
we are currently modelling them as the same item, as if a regular plate carrier
while coyote-colored carriers appear in online search, they are by and large a minuscule portion of all indexed carriers
until a conclusive proof of their widespread deployment in any given branch and/or conflict, we presume them to be exceedingly-rare and, therefore, not worthy of induction
description: The most-recent model of a plate carrier deployed by the U.S. Army prior to the Cataclysm.  It boasts a significant weight reduction compared to the previous model, as well as increased comfort, especially during long missions.  Provides ballistic protection against small-caliber rounds thanks to Kevlar inserts, and could be augmented further by adding hard ballistic plates.

variants.description: The newest model of a plate carrier employed by the U.S. Army prior to the Cataclysm, the Modular Scalable Vest boasts improved ergonomics over its predecessor, the IOTV, both through weight reduction and through comfort and fit improvements.  Offers ballistic protection against small-caliber rounds thanks to Kevlar inserts, and can be augmented with hard ballistic plates.  <variant:ocp>

//weight: it may be impossible to calculate the carrier's weight accurately from its purchase description, so we use a store source

notes: thickness is doubled to account for two sides → 0.27 in. × 2 = 0.54 in.

armor.//description: Base vest and straps, upper torso
armor.//coverage: extrapolated from IOTV coverage based on visual reference
extrapolated from IOTV coverage based on visual reference

pocket_data.description: Cummerbund Quad Release system attachment point

//sources: HighCom Trooper sa3100 soft armor insert
//notes: modelled after size M
we don't mention or model ID panels, pretending this is a generic, non-affiliated carrier
description mentions internal removable cummerbund, but no clear way to handle it, so we model the cummerbund as integrated
soft armor inside the carrier is modelled as integrated and NIJ IIIA
carrier has one round Velcro surface and one tiny panel Velcro surface
description: A plain ballistic vest with a variety of hook-and-loop surfaces for identification badges and panels.  Favored by law enforcement and federal agencies due to its simplicity and ergonomics.  Embedded soft armor protects against pistol-caliber rounds

variants.description: A plain-looking plate carrier.  Its ergonomic construction results in low encumbrance compared to the alternatives.  A variety of hook-and-loop surfaces could be used by identification panels.  <variant:black>

A plain-looking plate carrier.  Its ergonomic construction results in low encumbrance compared to the alternatives.  A variety of hook-and-loop surfaces could be used by identification panels.  <variant:navy>

//: the OCP variant doesn't exist explicitly for this carrier at the time of modelling these variants
the manufacturer states that other colors are available upon request
we model this variant temporarily in order to allow the National Guard to have a reasonable variant associated with them
as members of either Army or Air Force, they're more likely than not to sport OCP uniforms and in-field armor
